name: Brashard Smith
headline: Pass-catching specialist buried behind a rebuilt Chiefs backfield
body:
Smith's rookie line is the story of a depth piece, not a runner to build around. Across 17 games he played just a 16.7% snap share, turning 44 carries into 151 yards at a thin 3.43 per attempt with a -0.131 EPA per play and only 1.34 yards after contact. He never reached a 20-plus rush and broke zero tackles, so the ground game isn't the path. The encouraging signal is on routes: 25 catches on 35 targets for 172 yards, a receiving touchdown, and 1.76 yards per route run hint at a usable third-down skill set.

The 2026 picture got crowded fast. Kansas City brought in Kenneth Walker III to lead, then added Emari Demercado and rookie Emmett Johnson, pushing Smith toward the back of the depth chart. His value is contingent and narrow, essentially a receiving-role flier and a Walker handcuff. Outside an injury ahead of him, treat him as a deep-league name to monitor in camp, not a draftable asset.
